ch3_hub.text: You stand in the main central corridor (Sector A). Sector B is still dry and reachable this early — it will not stay that way. Gather evidence before the next lockdown. Where do you investigate?

choices.text: Check Miller's locker in the crew bay

ch3_security.text: You enter the Security Hub. David is glaring at a bank of monitors, sweating profusely. 'What do you want? I'm busy locking down the sectors.'
ch3_security.speaker: David

choices.text: Sneak a peek at his terminal.
Search the armory vents while he is distracted.

ch3_security_ask.text: 'The cameras in the lower decks are down. They've been down for two days. Probably corrosion. Nothing I can do about it now.' He refuses to make eye contact.
ch3_security_ask.speaker: David

ch3_security_terminal.text: While David is distracted by a comms ping, you glance at the main terminal log. You spot a Server Wipe Audit. David's credentials scrubbed all lower-deck camera feeds 48 hours ago.

ch3_security_clue_get.text: You quietly download the audit. David deleted the footage. Motive unknown — cover-up of a murder, or something smaller and uglier?

ch3_armory_vents.text: Behind a loose grate in the armory wall, you find a sealed packet of unprocessed Abyssal Quartz — packaged for smuggling, still damp with condensate.

choices.text: Photograph the stash and reseal the grate.
Leave it. Not your problem yet.

ch3_mess_hall.text: The Mess Hall is sealed with crime scene tape. The tipped-over pot of stew still stains the deck. Otto's inventory terminal blinks on the counter.

ch3_mess_inventory.text: The official ration manifest shows massive shortages of high-calorie stores — far more than spoilage or one greedy cook could explain.

choices.text: Copy the discrepancy report to your PDA.

ch3_mess_pantry.text: Behind a sack of synthetic flour, a crayon drawing is taped to the bulkhead: a dark shape with too many limbs, and a smiling stick figure labeled 'nice cook' handing out bowls.

choices.text: Leave it. Someone's idea of a joke.

ch3_dry_storage.text: In the back of dry storage, blankets and old coats form a small nest. It is far too short for Otto. A child's jacket hangs from a pipe, caked with abyssal mud.

choices.text: Close the door. Pretend you saw nothing.

ch3_medbay.text: Medbay is under partial biohazard seal. Through the glass you see Dr. Aris pacing, a datapad clutched to her chest. A rejected requisition printout is jammed under the door.

choices.text: Peer at the cold-storage labels through the glass.

ch3_medbay_requisition.text: Corporate stamp: DENIED. 'Too expensive. Treat with local supplies and keep the drill running.' Aris requested antivirals weeks ago — and was refused.

ch3_medbay_vials.text: Cold-storage tags visible through the frost: 'Severe Vitamin D Deficiency.' The fluid inside pulses with a faint green light. No vitamin deficiency does that.

choices.text: Step back. You have seen enough.

ch3_miller_locker.text: Miller's locker still smells of grease and ozone. Inside: a faded photo of a girl on a beach, crumpled hospital invoices, and a denied leave request stamped by Corporate.

ch3_miller_locker_more.text: Under the boots you find a fried relay switch — professional work. Someone silenced a structural alarm and left no secondary trip. Miller's tools. Miller's skill.

choices.text: Close the locker. You need air.

ch3_end.text: You retreat to your quarters to process what you found. Miller is dead. The cameras are wiped. Rations vanish into vents. Everyone has a secret — and the station is still falling apart. On the PA, Gideon's night bulletin is brief: remain in safe zones, trust the protocols, do not improvise. He sounds like a man reading a script written before the body cooled.

textVariants.text: You retreat to your quarters. Your hands shake when you try to type notes. Miller is dead. The cameras are wiped. Gideon's bulletin says trust the protocols. Somewhere behind your eyes, something listens to your thoughts and waits.

choices.text: Sleep and prepare for the next cycle.
